title: kardomia granitica
intro: Kardomia granitica, also known as Granite Kardomia or Granite Kumerahou, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myoporaceae. It is a shrub native to New Zealand, traditionally used by the indigenous Maori people for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. It can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its full range of health benefits and potential uses.
